question: How does Express route a request through its middleware stack?

graph.answer:
Express does this in layers, in insertion order:

- The exported `express()` app is just a function that calls `app.handle(req, res, next)` in [lib/express.js](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/express.js#L1).
- `app.handle()` sets up request/response plumbing, assigns `req.res`, `res.req`, swaps in the Express prototypes, initializes `res.locals`, and then delegates to `this.router.handle(req, res, done)` in [lib/application.js](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L152).
- Every `app.use()` and route registration adds a `Layer` to the router stack. Middleware and routes are both stack entries; the order you register them is the order they run in [lib/application.js](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L190) and [node_modules/router/index.js](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/node_modules/router/index.js#L398).
- `Router.prototype.handle()` walks `router.stack` with an internal `next()` function. For each layer it:
  - matches the request path,
  - runs `router.param()` callbacks first,
  - for plain middleware, strips the mounted prefix from `req.url` and updates `req.baseUrl`,
  - for route layers, checks the HTTP method and dispatches into the route’s own handler stack [node_modules/router/index.js](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/node_modules/router/index.js#L149).
- A `Route` is another small stack. `Route.prototype.dispatch()` iterates its handlers, selects handlers by method, and supports `next('route')` to skip the rest of the current route and `next('router')` to exit the router entirely [node_modules/router/lib/route.js](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/node_modules/router/lib/route.js#L98).
- Handler arity controls error flow: normal middleware has fewer than 4 args, error middleware has 4 args. `Layer.handleRequest()` skips error handlers, and `Layer.handleError()` skips normal handlers. Promise rejections are forwarded to `next(err)` [node_modules/router/lib/layer.js](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/node_modules/router/lib/layer.js#L106).
- If nothing handles the request, Express falls through to the final callback, which is `finalhandler` by default [lib/application.js](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/express/lib/application.js#L152).

In short: Express is a sequential stack walker. It matches path, then method, runs param hooks, strips mount prefixes for middleware, and advances only when a handler calls `next()`. If you want, I can also sketch the exact flow for `app.use('/x', ...)`, `router.get('/x', ...)`, and error middleware side by side.
